text.title: Account Health Scoring Model (Sub-20 Accounts) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Health dashboard runs on NPS alone; mid-tier accounts going red show as green.
antipattern.root-cause: NPS is lagging and infrequent at 3-20 account scale; one or two responses dominate.
antipattern.fix: Replace NPS-only with the 6-signal model; treat NPS as one secondary input.

antipattern.symptom: Founder manually overrides red scores on favourite accounts; churn lands a quarter later.
antipattern.root-cause: Manual override path exists; thresholds are mutable.
antipattern.fix: Lock thresholds in templates/signal-thresholds.md; require a documented exception with sign-off.

antipattern.symptom: Health scores stop updating because timesheet data lags 3 weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: No freshness check on the underlying signal sources.
antipattern.fix: compute-account-health.py MUST verify each signal source is ≤7 days old and flag stale sources.

antipattern.symptom: Account sits red for 6 weeks with no retention call placed.
antipattern.root-cause: Scoring runs but no escalation owner is assigned; trigger conditions documented but not actioned.
antipattern.fix: detect-escalation-trigger.py MUST emit a Telegram or email task to the named CS owner with due date.
